category_title: Timid students who feel voiceless and isolated in peer work
minimal_context: Brandon is standing in front of his 6th-grade science class to deliver a lesson as a co-teacher.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Brandon, please stand in front of the class and deliver the science lesson from the lesson plan.

STUDENT: (Stands in front of the class full of his peers) Okay...

TUTOR: Go ahead and teach them the science content.

STUDENT: (Talking slow) Today... we are... going to...

TUTOR: Keep trying.

STUDENT: (Very timid) I'm really nervous speaking in front of a class full of my peers. I'm talking slow... I usually feel like I have no voice because my partner doesn't like to talk to me.

options.A: Introduce the lesson by highlighting the importance of science, using scenarios, maintaining a rhythm for deep exploration, and providing feedback.
options.B: Establish a norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for the evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during the lesson.
options.C: Perform a quick review of the lesson plan with the student co-teacher to ensure that the science content is reflected accurately.
options.D: Implement a progressive disciplinary sequence: first a warning, then a 10-minute timeout, followed by classroom suspension, detention, or school suspension.
